title: Voter Import Tutorial
subtitle: Learn how to bulk-register voters for an election.

section_overview.heading: Overview
section_overview.body: The voter import feature lets election administrators register multiple voters in one step by uploading a CSV file. Depending on your organisation's membership mode, the import process works differently:

full_membership.label: Full Membership Mode
full_membership.description: Voters must already be active formal members of the organisation. The import links existing members to the election as voters.

election_only.label: Election-Only Mode
election_only.description: Voters can be new — the system auto-creates user accounts for unknown emails. Each new voter receives an invitation email to set their password and participate in the election.

section_requirements.heading: Who Can Be Imported as a Voter?
section_requirements.intro_full: In Full Membership Mode, the person must fulfil all three conditions:
section_requirements.intro_election: In Election-Only Mode, you have two options:
conditions_full: They must have a user account on the platform (a registered email address).
They must be a formal member of this organisation — present in the Members list.
Their membership status must be active (not expired, suspended, or ended).
conditions_election: EXISTING USERS: Platform members who are active organisation members will be automatically registered as voters.
NEW USERS: Unknown email addresses will be auto-created as new user accounts, and invitation emails will be sent with a password-setup link.
section_requirements.warning_full: Emails that do not meet all three conditions will be automatically skipped during import. No error will stop the import — valid rows are always processed.
section_requirements.warning_election: All valid email addresses will be processed. Existing users are linked to the election. New users receive invitation emails — be sure to check your email configuration before importing.

section_file_format.heading: CSV File Format
section_file_format.intro: The import file format depends on your membership mode:

format_full.title: Full Membership Mode
format_full.description: Simple format with email addresses only.
rules: The first row must be a header row: email
Each subsequent row must contain exactly one email address.
The file must be saved in UTF-8 encoding.
Supports both comma (,) and semicolon (;) delimiters.

format_election.title: Election-Only Mode
format_election.description: Extended format for name and email (new users get proper profiles).
rules: The first row must be a header row: firstname;lastname;email (semicolon-delimited)
Each subsequent row: First Name;Last Name;Email
The file must be saved in UTF-8 encoding.
Supports both comma and semicolon delimiters.
Names can be empty (email-only rows are accepted).

section_file_format.download_hint: Use the Download Template button on the import page to get the correct format for your organisation's membership mode.

steps_full.title: Step 1 — Prepare your CSV file
steps_full.body: Collect email addresses of organisation members who are active and should vote in this election. Download the template and fill it in.

steps_full.title: Step 2 — Upload the file
steps_full.body: On the import page, click "Select File" or drag and drop your CSV file into the upload area.

steps_full.title: Step 3 — Review the preview
steps_full.body: The system validates each row against membership status. Valid rows show ✅, invalid rows show ❌ with the reason. Invalid rows will be skipped — they do not block the import.

steps_full.title: Step 4 — Confirm and register
steps_full.body: Click "Import Voters". The system registers all valid voters for this election. You will see how many were registered, how many were already voters, and how many were skipped.

steps_election.title: Step 1 — Prepare your CSV file
steps_election.body: List all voters: existing members and new participants. Include first name, last name, and email. Download the template to ensure correct format.

steps_election.title: Step 2 — Upload the file
steps_election.body: On the import page, click "Select File" or drag and drop your CSV file into the upload area.

steps_election.title: Step 3 — Review the preview
steps_election.body: The system shows which users exist (marked 'existing') and which are new (marked 'will be created'). Verify everything looks correct before proceeding.

steps_election.title: Step 4 — Confirm and import
steps_election.body: Click "Import Voters". Existing users are registered as voters immediately. New users receive invitation emails with a password-setup link. You will see a summary of counts and how many invitation emails were sent.

steps_election.title: Step 5 — Check email delivery
steps_election.body: Monitor your email system to ensure invitation emails were delivered. New voters need the invitation email to set their password and vote.

items.question: What's the difference between Full Membership and Election-Only modes?
items.answer: Full Membership Mode requires voters to already be active formal members. Election-Only Mode allows auto-creating new voter accounts from the CSV — new voters receive invitation emails to set passwords. Check your organisation settings to see which mode is active.

items.question: What happens if an email is already registered as a voter?
items.answer: It is counted as 'already existing' and not duplicated. The import is safe to run multiple times.

items.question: What happens to new voters after import (Election-Only Mode)?
items.answer: New voters receive invitation emails with a link to set their password. They must complete the password setup before voting. The invitation expires after 7 days.

items.question: What happens if a person is a platform user but not a member (Full Membership Mode)?
items.answer: They will be skipped. Only active formal members with full voting rights can be registered as voters.

items.question: Can I import voters from outside the organisation?
items.answer: In Full Membership Mode: No, import is restricted to active members only. In Election-Only Mode: Yes, anyone with an email can be registered as a voter.

items.question: The import says 0 registered — what went wrong?
items.answer: Check: (1) CSV format matches your mode (email-only for Full, firstname;lastname;email for Election-Only), (2) Header row is correct, (3) In Full Mode, emails belong to active members.

items.question: Can I use a comma-separated CSV instead of semicolon?
items.answer: The system auto-detects the delimiter (comma or semicolon), so both formats are accepted.

items.question: Do I need to create member accounts first?
items.answer: In Full Membership Mode: Yes. In Election-Only Mode: No — the system auto-creates accounts for new emails.

items.question: What if an invitation email bounces or is not delivered?
items.answer: The voter is still registered but cannot set a password or vote without the email. Check your email configuration and resend invitations if needed.

seo.title: Voter Import Tutorial & Guide | Public Digit
seo.description: Master voter importing for elections. Complete guide on Full Membership vs Election-Only modes, CSV file formats, step-by-step import process, and how to bulk-register voters on Public Digit.
seo.keywords: voter import, election voters, CSV import, bulk import, voter registration, election setup, import tutorial, membership modes
seo.og_title: How to Import Voters for Elections | Public Digit Tutorial
seo.og_description: Learn the two ways to import voters: Full Membership Mode (existing users) and Election-Only Mode (auto-create new voters). Complete step-by-step tutorial with examples.
